title: podman hangs for an extended period of time when accessing container storage
body:
<!--
---------------------------------------------------
BUG REPORT INFORMATION
---------------------------------------------------
Use the commands below to provide key information from your environment:
You do NOT have to include this information if this is a FEATURE REQUEST

**NOTE** A large number of issues reported against Podman are often found to already be fixed
in more current versions of the project.  Before reporting an issue, please verify the
version you are running with `podman version` and compare it to the latest release
documented on the top of Podman's [README.md](../README.md).  If they differ, please
update your version of Podman to the latest possible and retry your command before creating
an issue.

If you are filing a bug against `podman build`, please instead file a bug
against Buildah (https://github.com/containers/buildah/issues). Podman build
executes Buildah to perform container builds, and as such the Buildah
maintainers are best equipped to handle these bugs.
-->

**Is this a BUG REPORT or FEATURE REQUEST? (leave only one on its own line)**

/kind bug

**Description**

When I attempt to run any podman command that accesses container storage, it hangs for several minutes before running.

**Steps to reproduce the issue:**

1. `podman images`

2. Wait for a while...

3. Keep waiting...

**Describe the results you received:**

It took a long time.

**Describe the results you expected:**

It should not take a long time.

**Additional information you deem important (e.g. issue happens only occasionally):**

I decided to `strace -f podman`, and after some filtering with grep I found this:

```
[pid 199378] openat(AT_FDCWD, "/var/home/ryan/.local/share/containers/storage/overlay-layers/layers.lock", O_RDWR|O_CREAT, 0600) = 9

**Output of `podman version`:**

```
Version:            1.8.0
RemoteAPI Version:  1
Go Version:         go1.14rc1
OS/Arch:            linux/amd64
```

comments.body: @nalind PTAL
@refi64, can you reproduce consistently or does it appear at seemingly random times?

@vrothberg Using https://github.com/jayaddison/grocy-docker/tree/b78b1810ff159e583a8770694305e2e5472ddb76 results in some kind of blocked-process condition during `podman play kube grocy.yaml` under the following local working environment:

```

```

Local images can be built from a copy of this tree by running:

```sh
# Produce grocy-app:libpod-issue-5631 and grocy-nginx:libpod-issue-5631 images
make build
```

The stall-point doesn't seem consistent between playthroughs; roughly speaking the following steps are being used between each attempt:

```sh
# Clear state
podman pod stop -a; podman container rm -a ; podman volume rm -a ; podman pod rm -a

# Attempt playthrough (with system-call tracing via strace)
strace podman play kube grocy.yaml
```

(and to add context and clarify my previous comment: the aforementioned stalled process condition is reliably reproducible, and *sometimes* occurs following an `openat` operations on `.local/share/containers/storage/overlay**/*.lock` files; the system calls prior to the stall seem inconsistent so there may be a race condition involved)
Removing the `volumes` and `volumeMounts` configuration keys from the source YAML allows the containers to start, and that seems to confirm the notion that this issue is storage-access-related.

Note that https://github.com/jayaddison/grocy-docker/blob/b78b1810ff159e583a8770694305e2e5472ddb76/grocy.yaml#L44-L49 contains multiple `tmpfs` mounts.

Reducing this to a single mount entry removes the hang condition and allows the `kubectl play kube` process to complete and the containers (and pod) to start.

Is it possible this line has something to do with the issue?

https://github.com/containers/libpod/blob/v1.8.2/pkg/adapter/pods.go#L689

To a naive reading (I'm not hugely familiar with Go) it looks like that could lead to the [three named mounts](https://github.com/jayaddison/grocy-docker/blob/b78b1810ff159e583a8770694305e2e5472ddb76/grocy.yaml#L44-L49) collapsing down to a single key under `volumes`.
Confirmed: after providing unique distinguishing `name` values for the three mounts, the problem is successfully worked-around and the `kubectl play kube` step succeeds.
@vrothberg I can reproduce it entirely consistently, as in, every single call that accesses the storage reproduces the issue across multiple reboots :sweat_smile:

Reading back on the original issue report here, it sounds like the problem I'm experiencing is fairly different.

I'll create a separate issue to track what I now understand regarding the issue concerning mountpoints & volume names.

Hey! In my case I have seen the same behaviour for a couple of days, but after some time,
It disappeared...and I can't reproduce it anymore from now.

@giuseppe, could this be fuse-overlayfs? I have `v0.7.8` on my machine.

> @giuseppe, could this be fuse-overlayfs? I have `v0.7.8` on my machine.

`podman images` should not mount any image



> @vrothberg I can reproduce it entirely consistently, as in, every single call that accesses the storage reproduces the issue across multiple reboots sweat_smile

are there other podman processes running?
A friendly reminder that this issue had no activity for 30 days.
@giuseppe @vrothberg @refi64  Did this problem go away or was the issue lost in the flow of working on other stuff?

> are there other podman processes running?
